elements.2412-15285_sent_d3b9421e: Fifth (final): major contributions and/or insights.} \end{comment} Large language models (LLM) are typically pretrained on large amounts of data in the order of billions (B) or trillions (T) of tokens derived from multiple data sources such as web crawl, books, papers, patents, mathematical and legal documents, and so forth~\cite{brown2020languagemodelsfewshotlearners,parmar2024nemotron415btechnicalreport,gemmateam2024gemmaopenmodelsbased,dubey2024llama,nvidia2024nemotron4340btechnicalreport}.
elements.2412-15285_sent_63fabf70: To develop a state-of-the-art model, it is critical to understand the nature of these data sources and to make informed decisions about optimal data blending (how different data sources are weighed during pretraining) and training strategies.
elements.2412-15285_sent_b9ec7bf1: These decisions typically involve running multiple large-scale experiments to empirically investigate the optimal training data blend(s) and ordering of data.
elements.2412-15285_sent_f71fa0ad: Most advanced models~\cite{gpt4-openai2024gpt4technicalreport,llama3-dubey2024llama3herdmodels} do not divulge information on the data blends that are used, nor the ablation studies informing the data mixing and ordering decisions.
elements.2412-15285_sent_9f669ec3: Recent works \cite{domain-upsampling-blakeney2024doesdatasparkjoy,olmo-groeneveld-etal-2024-olmo,llama3-dubey2024llama3herdmodels,snowflake-arctic} provide high-level data blend information about a small portion of pretraining by encouraging the upsampling of certain domains towards the end.
elements.2412-15285_sent_4df88cd5: In general, there exists a knowledge gap regarding how to craft and choose an optimal data blend(s) for the entire training process, and the generalizability of data blends and ordering strategies to larger token horizons and model sizes.

elements.2412-15285_sent_111eda76: In this work, we explore a two-phased approach to pretraining: phase-1 (\phaseone) then phase-2 (\phasetwo).
elements.2412-15285_sent_843fdf58: In each phase, we explore different data blends based on the quality and number of epochs to be seen of a data source.
elements.2412-15285_sent_871955f1: In phase-1 (\phaseone), we explore a general data distribution which consists of a mix of web crawl data, medium-quality data, and low amounts of high-quality data.
elements.2412-15285_sent_7808e191: In phase-2 (\phasetwo), we explore a blend which includes task data and emphasizes high-quality datasets such as math, code, and high-quality web crawl (\S\ref{subsec:crawl_data_mix}).
elements.2412-15285_sent_3ce4ef40: As seen in Figure~\ref{fig:train_pipeline}, our model sees the first general data blend during \phaseone for the majority of training, then a different data blend focused on high quality data during the shorter \phasetwo of training.
elements.2412-15285_sent_9676e7d9: The steps to create blends for \phaseone and \phasetwo are: 1) Downsample a data source by a factor of $f$, 2) Estimate the quality of a data source (\S\ref{subsec:crawl_data_mix}), 3) Estimate the epochs to be seen in the whole pretraining (\S\ref{subsec:epoch_results}) and finally 4) distribute the epochs appropriately in \phaseone and \phasetwo (\S\ref{sec:data-blends}).
elements.2412-15285_sent_c1ba8cc9: The downsampling factor $f$ is based on the final total token budget which we assume to be $15$T similar to \citet{llama3-dubey2024llama3herdmodels}.

elements.2412-15285_sent_b0d6628b: While previous work~\citep{dubey2024llama,yang2024qwen2,team2024gemma} mentions that web crawl documents like Common Crawl (CC) form a large majority of their pretraining data, none of them share a recipe on how to mix different slices of CC.
elements.2412-15285_sent_0e13caf6: Some recent work on constructing crawl-based pretraining datasets ~\citep{penedo2024fineweb,li2024datacomp} directly use the high quality crawl documents in pretraining but provide no specific data mixing strategy.
elements.2412-15285_sent_3f9f4c70: In this section, we provide comprehensive details on how to create a data blend for CC documents and use it effectively in our phase-1 and phase-2 of pretraining.

elements.2412-15285_sent_d48621b9: Each document in our web crawl data is classified into one of five quality categories: High, Medium-High, Medium, Medium-Low, and Low using the classifier from \citet{su2024nemotroncctransformingcommoncrawl}.
elements.2412-15285_sent_a07a9b00: We investigate various blends using quality-based weighted sampling approach\footnote{We show comparison of quality-based blending with natural token distribution-based blend in \S\ref{app:quality-based-blend}.} for all of web crawl data from 99 CC snapshots for our phase-1 of pretraining.
elements.2412-15285_sent_13834676: The idea is to upsample high and medium-quality crawl documents while avoiding a high quantity of low-quality data.
elements.2412-15285_sent_462798c3: The overall idea for the four web crawl blends in Table~\ref{table: crawl_data_mixing_phase1} is to iteratively decrease the percentage of tokens from High and Medium-High and increase the tokens in the lower categories.

elements.2412-15285_sent_7386b388: Selecting and structuring pretraining datasets is important to improve model generalization and efficiency.
elements.2412-15285_sent_44e68154: \citet{llama3-dubey2024llama3herdmodels} emphasize openness and accessibility of models, \citet{together2023redpajama,soldaini-etal-2024-dolma} assemble an open corpus of trillions of tokens for large-scale training, and \citet{olmo-groeneveld-etal-2024-olmo} release a truly Open Language Model, including its framework, training data, and code.
elements.2412-15285_sent_300ab623: Studies such as \citet{li2024datacomplm,fineweb-penedo2024the} demonstrating that refined data selection impacts model accuracies more significantly than simply the quantity of data.
elements.2412-15285_sent_5466ddef: But these studies are primarily aimed at CC data and they do not suggest any data mixing strategies for pretraining.
elements.2412-15285_sent_37441324: \citet{parmar-etal-2024-data} provide a systematic approach to building effective LLM pretraining datasets with ablations on data attributes, and existing curation, selection, and sampling methods.
elements.2412-15285_sent_a7fbfad0: In our work, we provide a systematic approach to craft data blends and to order the data in pretraining.
elements.2412-15285_sent_efd784ae: Strategic weighting and timing of data usage can also noticeably impact model accuracies.
elements.2412-15285_sent_fe86449f: Techniques like domain upsampling \cite{domain-upsampling-blakeney2024doesdatasparkjoy,llama3-dubey2024llama3herdmodels} towards the end of training have been shown to be effective.
elements.2412-15285_sent_4ab1aeed: \citet{snowflake-arctic,olmo-groeneveld-etal-2024-olmo} provide details about high level blends for their pretraining process.
elements.2412-15285_sent_8d6bc210: In contrast, our work provides fine grained details about the data blend creation process along with actionable steps that model developers can use to develop data blends and order.
elements.2412-15285_sent_954080a1: Prior work \cite{shen2023slimpajama,longpre2024pretrainer,pmlr-v162-mindermann22a,doremi-NEURIPS2023_dcba6be9,xie2023data,shao2024balanced} investigates optimizing data mixtures based on clustering methods, manually designed domain composition weights, proxy models or reference models to determine data composition weights and sample-level data selection.
elements.2412-15285_sent_d4e396f1: Our work primarily focuses on data ordering and scaling of data blends in pretraining and can be used in conjunction with other data sampling techniques.
elements.2412-15285_sent_160c2a47: Curriculum learning approaches inspired by human learning offer an ordered way to introduce data gradually to enhance model learning.
elements.2412-15285_sent_e2b4aafe: \citet{martinez-etal-2023-climb,curriculum-survey,feng-etal-2024-child} investigate cognitively-motivated curriculum-based training including vocabulary, and objective curricula, and outline and the challenges and potential solutions for designing effective curricula.
elements.2412-15285_sent_f7416fe3: Our work shows that ordering of data based on quality in pretraining LLMs has a significant impact of downstream accuracies.
elements.2412-15285_sent_b38623f1: In conclusion, through extensive experiments, we demonstrate the effectiveness of a two-phase pretraining approach for LLM.
elements.2412-15285_sent_62ce7c47: For the initial training phase, a more general data distribution consisting of mainly of web crawl proves most effective, while phase two benefits from a comprehensive data blend, with additional focus on math, code, and task data.
elements.2412-15285_sent_e7a86e31: Phase-two for the last $\approx$40\% of training yields the best results, and over-extending it leads to diminishing returns.
elements.2412-15285_sent_2e4aae53: Increasing model size and token horizon further enhances accuracy, demonstrating the scalability of our approach.
elements.2412-15285_sent_fd7beaae: Importantly, we also show that considering both the quality of the data (including web crawl) and the number of epochs of each data source is crucial to attain optimal results and prevent overfitting.
elements.2412-15285_sent_f4c77c3c: \begin{comment} Future work can investigate potential effects of two-phase training other than overfitting, e.g. impacts on bias \& fairness.
elements.2412-15285_sent_7675f9b4: Further, it would be interesting to see how two-phase training can be applied to niche domains or applications, e.g. medicine or finance, or a multimodal setting, e.g. vision-language models (VLM).
elements.2412-15285_sent_9cebe53f: Some limitations of our work include our present suite of models and evaluation benchmarks.
elements.2412-15285_sent_0a681786: We can extend our work and show the effectiveness of two-phase pretraining approach on more LLM architectures such as Mamba~\cite{gu2023mamba}, other hybrid SSM based architectures~\cite{glorioso2024zamba,lieber2024jamba} and mixture of experts~\cite{shazeer2017outrageously}.
elements.2412-15285_sent_977d0d11: While our evaluation benchmarks are quite comprehensive, we could potentially expand to an even broader range of evaluations, including nuanced domain-specific or interactive tasks, or more theory of mind and developmental psychology-inspired benchmarks.
elements.2412-15285_sent_f82a15d8: This includes assessing capabilities such as analogical reasoning \cite{webb2023emergentanalogicalreasoninglarge}.
elements.2412-15285_sent_9aa31ca8: Further, our scaling experiments could be expanded. Scaling up to hundreds of billions of parameters or significantly longer training may yield additional insights.
elements.2412-15285_sent_3365a161: Lastly, while our work focuses on two-phase training and shows its efficacy, we can potentially investigate multi-phase training, and the impact of the order of the phases.
elements.2412-15285_sent_fdc1e5fe: However, we believe this is more suited for future work.
elements.2412-15285_sent_58cafaf6: Overall, these are directions to potentially improve and expand upon our work.
elements.2412-15285_sent_8004dcce: Despite these potential limitations, we feel that our current work is an insightful and useful contribution to the research community.
elements.2412-15285_sent_1e07efb1: Our research uses publicly available and commonly used datasets in LLM development.
elements.2412-15285_sent_d08fb1d1: These sources, including Common Crawl, Wikipedia, and code repositories, are widely adopted in the research community.
elements.2412-15285_sent_4ccabee4: We examined the quality and origins of our data, prioritizing high-quality, domain-relevant data sources to improve LLM capabilities in a responsible manner.
elements.2412-15285_sent_36ae799d: However, web crawl data may inherently contain biases or inappropriate content despite filtering efforts.
elements.2412-15285_sent_fe79add0: We used established data cleaning and quality assurance procedures but acknowledge that potential biases may persist and impact model behavior in certain circumstances.
elements.2412-15285_sent_173432a6: We recognize that scaling models and exploring data blending strategies require significant computational resources, which may raise environmental concerns.
elements.2412-15285_sent_004f1fb8: To mitigate this, we focused on efficient training strategies, such as two-phase training, to improve accuracy without excessively increasing resource usage.
elements.2412-15285_sent_e3af265a: Future studies could benefit from exploring energy-efficient training methods to further minimize the environmental impact.
elements.2412-15285_sent_6fea60cb: Our models, data blends, and accompanying publication are intended solely for research purposes, with no intended real-world application without additional safety evaluations.
elements.2412-15285_sent_b3e1e239: We caution against deploying models based on our methods without thorough testing, as they may carry unknown risks, particularly when applied to tasks involving sensitive or personal information.
elements.2412-15285_sent_8374d352: Our work aims to advance the understanding of LLM training strategies, and we feel that it is an important contribution to the research community.
elements.2412-15285_sent_fcab2141: We encourage researchers to expand upon our work while further investigating the ethical and societal implications of LLM.
elements.2412-15285_sent_748df00d: We use RoPE position embeddings \cite{rope-paper}, RMSNorm layer normalization \cite{RMSNorm}, with Grouped Query Attention \cite{ainslie-etal-2023-gqa}.
elements.2412-15285_sent_4a3dc3a4: We use a global batch size of 1536, and the Adam optimizer \cite{kingma2017adammethodstochasticoptimization} with $\beta=(0.9,0.95)$ and $\epsilon=1e$-$08$.
elements.2412-15285_sent_e0cfbd89: \phaseone training uses cosine $LR$ decay with an initial $LR$ of $3e$-$4$ and targeted to reach a min-$LR$ of $3e$-$6$ at the end of the full training run (both phases).
elements.2412-15285_sent_b82b91e5: We start \phasetwo with the intermediate $LR$ reached at the end of \phaseone, and anneal using cosine $LR$ decay to $3e$-$6$ (\S\ref{app:lr_schedule}).
elements.2412-15285_sent_e39b80c4: Our experiments are run using up to 1024 NVIDIA H100 GPUs.
elements.2412-15285_section_be16b462: Detailed Two-Phase Pretraining Results (Reasoning, MMLU, Code)

elements.2412-15285_sent_3d2e3ee2: We first compare a baseline blend (ND) which uses the natural distribution of tokens with a smartly constructed weighted sampling blend (WS).
elements.2412-15285_sent_d23a5780: ND is based on the number of tokens that belong in each category as opposed to utilizing the quality label i.e. if 59\% of the tokens belong to Low then 59\% of tokens seen during pretraining would belong to Low.
elements.2412-15285_sent_7c89d5f6: We then create a data blend (WS) based on weighted sampling of high and medium-quality tokens.
elements.2412-15285_sent_94896fe3: The idea is to upsample high and medium-quality crawl documents and not use the low-quality data at all.

elements.2412-15285_sent_f300d8b9: Table~\ref{table: crawl_data_mixing_comp} illustrates the results of the two models trained on the ND and WS data blends of web crawl, respectively.
elements.2412-15285_sent_98b383e9: We see that our data blend (WS) outperforms on most of the evaluation tasks by a large margin, and the improvement on MMLU is substantial.

elements.2412-15285_sent_44c31b65: We investigate different learning rate ($LR$) schedules for phase \phasetwo.

elements.2412-15285_sent_86983091: We compare cosine vs. linear $LR$ decay functions, and also compare decaying to a final $LR$ of $0$ vs. $3e$-$6$ (1\% of the original \phaseone starting $LR$ of $3e$-$4$).
elements.2412-15285_sent_86b7f20c: Not decaying $LR$ entirely to 0 leaves room for post-training, which is likely preferable.
elements.2412-15285_sent_6e3cbbb1: As seen in Table \ref{tab:LR-anneal-results}, there is a negligible difference between linear and cosine $LR$ decay, so we choose cosine decay for consistency with \phaseone.

elements.2412-15285_sent_894827bf: Hence, our final chosen annealing strategy is cosine $LR$ decay to $3e$-$6$, which we use for our final two-phase experiments in \S\ref{subsec:two-phase-results}.
